Indications at Dawn and Twilight XXX 287
The following things being glossy at & juncture, betoken imme-
diate rain : Danda (a roddike formation caused by the combination
of sun-beams, clouds and wind), lightning, fish-like formations of
clouds, a mock sun, a halo, a rainbow, Airdvata (a long fragmentary
rainbow) and the sun's rays.
[There are many technical terms in this stanza. For Danda
vide verse 16 infra; for Paridhi, and Airdvata vide XLVII 19.20; for
Parivefa vide XXXIV-1.]
The Sun's rays, which are broken off, unequal, discoloured,
unnatural, crooked, turned anti-clockwise, slender, short, impaired
and soiled (not bright) cause war and drought.
[In the second line there is a metrical error as in is
taken as a simple consonant while it is actually a conjunct one.
According to Pifigala it is optionally treated as single consonant,]
